key_insight: A graph neural network that models longitudinal breast MRI timepoints, plus self-supervised trajectory learning, can better predict neoadjuvant chemotherapy response than standard baselines.
clinical_relevance: More accurate pCR prediction could help clinicians better estimate which breast cancer patients are likely to benefit from neoadjuvant chemotherapy and support more individualized treatment planning.
method: Built a 3D spatio-temporal imaging framework for serial DCE-MRI scans.
Used graph neural network relational modeling to capture interactions across timepoints.
Added three self-supervised objectives to learn treatment-trajectory representations without labels.
evidence_signal: In 585 ISPY-2 patients, the method outperformed vision and self-supervised baselines across multiple classification metrics, with ablation studies examining timepoints and inter-scan time differences.
watch_item: Performance was evaluated on a specific public cohort, so external validation and consistent availability of multiple MRI timepoints may be needed before broad clinical use.
paper_title: Graph Representation Learning of Longitudinal Medical Imaging Trajectories for Treatment Response Prediction
